title: Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Youmay|the battle during the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]]|the [[Battle of Kirrek (Great Hyperspace War)|battle]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]}} {{Battle |prev=[[First battle between Jedi and Sith]][[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] |conc= |next=[[Battle of Khar Delba]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]] |image=[[File:Bkir.jpg]] |name=Battle of Kirrek |conflict=[[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]] |date=[[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] |place=[[Kirrek]] |result=[[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros]] victory |side1=Koros Army |side2=Kirrek Rebellion |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] *[[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] *[[Memit Nadill]] |commanders2=[[Llaban]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*1 [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] *1 [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] *[[Zeta Group]] **[[Starfighter/Legends|Koros Spaceworks starbombers]] *Koros soldiers *''[[Shadow Runner]]'' |forces2=[[Kirrek rebels]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*[[Hok Daragon]] *[[Timar Daragon]] |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Your cause has failed. Your violence is overthrown. You have lost.|Odan-Urr, during the Battle of Kirrek|}} This '''Battle of Kirrek''' took place in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], during the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]]. Having unified six of the seven [[Planet/Legends|planets]] in the Koros [[Star system/Legends|system]], the political leader [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] ordered a conquest of the only remaining independent world, [[Kirrek]]. However, an advance contingent of Teta's [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] was besieged by an [[Army/Legends|army]] of Kirrek rebels and forced to take shelter in a hastily-assembled citadel. After a month, Teta ordered a full-on military assault, led by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Memit Nadill]] and [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]]. Using the [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] known as [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], the Jedi were able to lead Teta's forces to victory, and the Koros system was unified. ==Prelude== {{Quote|The trade alliance benefits all worlds in this system–but these rebels prefer to live in squalor, without laws!|Empress Teta|}} In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], a political leader in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] named [[Empress/Legends|Empress]]{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}} [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] began a [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|war]] to unify the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] seven [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. Teta hoped to unite the worlds in mutual commerce and aid and to set up a system-wide government. Teta's forces managed to unify six of the seven worlds; the only independent planet in the Koros system remaining was [[Kirrek]]. Kirrek did not have a true organized military, only a loose assortment of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and rebels led by a [[Gender/Legends|man]] named [[Llaban]]. ==The battle== {{Quote|We've got no chance–but we can take some of them with us to our graves! Never surrender, I say! Fire on the weakest targets–kill as many as you can!|Unidentified member of the Kirrek army|}} [[File:MemitBattleMed.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Empress Teta's warriors during the Battle of Kirrek]] To conquer Kirrek, the Tetan forces began by landing an advance contingent of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] on the planet; however, the rebel guerrilla forces fought well, trapping the Tetan [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] within the walls of a citadel that the rebels besieged. Much-needed reinforcements were not able to reach the soldiers, as the rebels had set up a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] and took to attacking any incoming [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. The citadel was besieged for more than a month, and the rebels responded to any attempts to assist the soldiers by [[Death/Legends|slaughtering]] hostages. Due to the dire situation that the Tetan soldiers were in, many starship crews found the opportunity to make [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] by running supplies to the soldiers in their personal craft. One such ship was the ''[[Shadow Runner]]'', operated by [[Hok Daragon|Hok]] and [[Timar Daragon]]. After more than a month, the Tetan forces added a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to their ranks—[[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], a [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]] scholar who had never seen battle before. Odan-Urr joined another Jedi, Teta's personal advisor, [[Memit Nadill]], in an attempt to break the blockade and relieve the besieged soldiers. Although Teta feared that a full-on military assault would turn out to be a [[Blood/Legends|bloodbath]], Odan-Urr provided a solution—an exotic [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] known as [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], which could raise the morale of Teta's soldiers and make the Kirrek rebels lose the will to fight. Odan-Urr and Nadill put this power to use during the assault with great success, demoralizing the rebels and pressing them into a retreat. On the other side of the battle, the besieged soldiers were overjoyed upon sighting the Tetan forces coming to their rescue. However, the battle meditation resulted in an unexpected side-effect, releasing the last reserves of desperation in the rebels and causing them to begin firing on weaker targets in an attempt to take as many of them down before their own defeat. This caused the destruction of many supply ships coming to the aid of the Tetan soldiers, which weren't as heavily armored as the military ships. Ultimately, however, the Tetan forces were victorious, with the battle meditation saving many lives and preventing a particularly bloody battle. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|We've won…but it was terrible.''"
"''No, Odan-Urr–this could have been a bloodbath, but your battle meditation saved many lives here. Never forget that.''"
"''Tell it to those poor souls in that supply ship…I never want to see war again.|Odan-Urr and Memit Nadill, referring to the destroyed ''Shadow Runner''|}} With the Tetan victory in the Battle of Kirrek, the seven worlds of the Koros system were united at last. Armed resistance continued for a time on Kirrek and other worlds, but the planets ultimately failed to free themselves. Teta was installed as the Empress of the whole Koros system, which was eventually renamed as the "Empress Teta system." One of the supply ships destroyed in the battle was the ''Shadow Runner'', and after the battle, the Daragons' children, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], were given the Daragons' other ship, the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]''. This would prove to be a pivotal moment in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|history]], as the Daragons would take a blind [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump in the ''Starbreaker 12'' and discover the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], sparking the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Kirrek made its first and only appearance in [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]], the prologue [[Star Wars comic books|issue]] to the ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'' arc. It would later be mentioned in passing in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]-exclusive feature ''[[Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji]]'', which was the first to name the battle. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]] {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{C|Mentioned in Codex entry}} ==Sources== *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}} {{1stID|Battle of Kirrek}} *{{DB|character|aarrba|Aarrba the Hutt}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Erste Schlacht von Kirrek#legends |es=Batalla de Kirrek (Guerras de Unificación) |fi=Kirrekin taistelu (yhdistymissodat) |fr=Bataille de Kirrek |pl=Pierwsza bitwa o Kirrek }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kirrek (Unification Wars), Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Kirrek]] [[Category:Battles of the Unification Wars]]
